name: ‘Not a fad’: UNLV law school to launch AI use class this fall
summary: First-year UNLV law students will be required to take a new class on artificial intelligence uses in legal practices starting this fall, the college announced Tuesday.

name: Can X-rays save your EV battery? A UNLV professor is extending the life of tech
summary: A UNLV professor is looking to an unlikely source to extend the life of the lithium-ion batteries that have become a part of everyday life: X-rays.

name: UNLV study finds hospital sewage samples offer early warning for deadly fungus
summary: Sampling wastewater near hospitals and care facilities can detect a deadly, drug-resistant fungus up to five months before patients show symptoms, according to new UNLV research.

name: These 6 Kitchen Tools Are Dirtier Than You Think, According to Food Safety Experts
summary: Thanks to moist environments (like your kitchen sink) and food particles, the kitchen is full of objects that make a perfect breeding ground for bacteria. So how can you ensure you aren’t spreading germs the next time you serve a meal? According to the food safety experts we spoke with, it starts with knowing where to look—and giving some often-overlooked tools a thorough cleaning. The items below are some of the most surprising offenders.

name: Passenger points out strange detail to flight attendant—has instant regret
summary: Dan Bubb, a former airline pilot who is an aviation historian at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas, told Newsweek that the issue visible in the viral video appears to involve a part of the wing’s spoiler.

name: Charity Qualifies for Sales Tax Exemption, Nevada Justices Rule
summary: A nonprofit formed to help the University of Nevada-Las Vegas build a medical school is exempt from sales and use tax as a charitable organization, regardless of the fact that it partnered with the government, the state high court ruled.

name: On the Etymology of the Term ‘Paramedic’
summary: The origin of the term “paramedic” is from the military. Medics, often called corpsmen, were trained to care for injured military personnel in the battlefield setting and in field hospitals. Despite the rumors, the term paramedic had nothing to do with parachutes. It simply meant that they were “non-doctors” (paraprofessionals) who provided medical care in the military setting.

name: What Tilman Fertitta's pending purchase of Caesars Entertainment means for Las Vegas
summary: The much-anticipated move would give Fertitta control of one of the largest gaming companies in the world and put him in direct competition with MGM.
